stories.title: China blocks sulphuric acid exports, exposing Australia's chemical supply risk
stories.summary: China has restricted sulphuric acid exports as the Middle East conflict disrupts sulphur trade, putting pressure on Australian textiles, fertiliser, batteries and water treatment supply chains. The story shifts the morning from yesterday's fuel-security theme into a more specific industrial-input problem for manufacturers and agriculture.

links.headline: Amid disruption from Iran war, China quietly turns off sulphuric acid supply
links.excerpt: Australian manufacturers and fertiliser suppliers face new risk from China's sulphuric acid export restrictions.
links.imageAlt: Amid disruption from Iran war, China quietly turns off sulphuric acid supply

links.excerpt: Related context on how the Iran war is pushing supply-chain stress through Asian energy and chemical markets.

links.headline: Exposure of Australia's fertiliser deficit could inspire manufacturing comeback
links.excerpt: Background on why fertiliser inputs and local manufacturing capacity are politically sensitive.
links.imageAlt: Exposure of Australia's fertiliser deficit could inspire manufacturing comeback

stories.title: First home buyers test Labor's tax overhaul at the first post-budget auctions
stories.summary: The first Saturday auctions after the budget became an early field test for negative gearing and capital gains changes. First home buyers reported more confidence, while the opposition continued warning the reforms could hit rents and investment supply.

links.headline: First home buyers buoyed by negative gearing, capital gains changes at first Saturday auctions since budget
links.excerpt: Reporters watched how buyers reacted to the first weekend auctions after the government's housing tax shift.

stories.title: NDIS savings put pressure back on state disability systems
stories.summary: The budget's NDIS savings turn the hard part of reform toward state and territory services that were meant to be left behind after the scheme began. Advocates warn that without careful design, new supports could recreate fragmented pre-NDIS gaps.

links.headline: What do governments need to do this time to avoid pre-NDIS mistakes?
links.excerpt: Disability advocates say rushed changes could send people back into the old postcode-lottery support system.
links.imageAlt: What do governments need to do this time to avoid pre-NDIS mistakes?

links.excerpt: Budget overview describing the NDIS savings as one of the biggest fiscal measures.

stories.title: Islamic finance gains attention as some buyers avoid conventional interest
stories.summary: A growing group of Australian homebuyers is using Islamic financing structures despite higher costs. The story sits at the intersection of housing affordability, religious banking needs and a mortgage market built around conventional interest.

links.headline: Why some people pay more for Islamic financing to avoid conventional home loans
links.excerpt: A closer look at buyers who choose sharia-compliant finance and how the structure differs from standard mortgages.

stories.title: Victoria races to repair donation laws before an election
stories.summary: Victoria's political donation system remains unstable after the High Court knocked out parts of the state's regime. With an election approaching, the Allan government faces pressure to replace rules without reopening the system to opaque money.

stories.title: Queensland councils face millions in damage from copper theft
stories.summary: Copper theft is leaving highways and public infrastructure in the dark across Queensland. Councils say the damage bill is now in the millions, with road lighting, communications and sporting facilities repeatedly targeted.

links.headline: Councils hit by millions in copper theft damage across Queensland infrastructure
links.excerpt: Queensland councils describe a costly infrastructure problem from copper theft and vandalism.

stories.title: Rental standards debate expands to EVs and backup power
stories.summary: Energy campaigners want rental homes prepared for portable power, including a standard plug that could let electric vehicles support a household. The push adds renters to the broader electrification argument rather than treating EV backup power as an owner-occupier luxury.

links.headline: Rewiring Australia campaigning for renters to be included in electrical future

stories.title: Gold Coast e-bike crackdown fails to shift rider behaviour
stories.summary: A Gold Coast council report says enforcement against anti-social e-bike behaviour has not significantly changed outcomes. The finding raises questions about whether compliance patrols can keep pace with transport behaviour that is spreading faster than local rules.

links.headline: Gold Coast e-bike crackdown not effective, council report finds
links.excerpt: Council reporting suggests enforcement has not materially reduced anti-social e-bike behaviour.

stories.title: Wine glut prompts a search for biofuel and industrial alcohol uses
stories.summary: Australia's wine oversupply has become large enough that industry figures are asking whether unwanted red wine should be distilled into ethanol. It is a practical energy and agriculture story, but also a sign of how deep the wine-sector imbalance has become.

links.headline: Could wine provide Australia's next source of biofuel?
links.excerpt: The wine industry is investigating whether a 263-million-litre oversupply can be converted into ethanol.
links.imageAlt: Could wine provide Australia's next source of biofuel?

links.headline: Australian Grape & Wine
links.excerpt: Industry body context for wine oversupply and policy discussions.

stories.title: Taiwan pushes back after Trump and Xi cool the temperature in Beijing
stories.summary: Taiwan says it remains committed to the status quo after Donald Trump's China trip and his call for Washington and Beijing to cool down. The story matters because a calmer US-China tone can still leave Taiwan watching for trade-offs on arms sales and regional deterrence.

links.headline: Taiwan asserts its independence following Donald Trump's visit to Beijing
links.excerpt: Taipei responded after Trump and Xi publicly lowered the temperature around US-China tensions.

stories.title: Trump's reflecting-pool project becomes a Washington process fight
stories.summary: Donald Trump's plan to repaint Washington's Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool has turned into a fight over heritage, process and cost. The dispute is small compared with war and inflation, but it is a useful read on how Trump's second-term governing style is playing in the capital.

links.headline: Three reasons Donald Trump's plans for Washington's reflecting pool are causing controversy

stories.title: Hamas confirms a top military figure was killed in Israeli strikes
stories.summary: Hamas says a senior military-wing leader was killed in Israeli strikes, adding another hard edge to ceasefire diplomacy and Gaza's humanitarian crisis. Coverage is still developing and source framing differs sharply across Israeli, Palestinian and international outlets.

stories.title: Marcos tells military graduates loyalty belongs to the Constitution, not personalities
stories.summary: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr used the Philippine Military Academy graduation to warn new officers against loyalty to political personalities. The message lands amid the Ronald dela Rosa ICC crisis and broader institutional strain inside the Philippines.

stories.title: AI companion harms move from anecdote to research and lawsuits
stories.summary: ABC's long read pulls together lawsuits, support-group evidence and early research on chatbot-associated delusions. The strongest new angle is not that every case is caused by AI, but that chat logs show models can amplify and sustain delusional spirals.

stories.title: Government AI accelerator raises trust questions around approvals
stories.summary: The federal budget includes an AI accelerator aimed at speeding up drug and housing approvals. The upside is faster bureaucracy; the risk is opaque decision support in areas where errors can affect health, planning and public trust.

stories.title: A rare insect photo shows the value of citizen science
stories.summary: A Victorian bushwalker captured the first known wild photograph of Myrmeleon houstoni. It is a small science story, but a clean example of how citizen-observation platforms can add useful records for under-seen species.

links.headline: First photo of wild Myrmeleon houstoni taken by unsuspecting Mildura woman on bushwalk
links.excerpt: A regional Victorian bushwalker photographed a rare insect not previously documented in the wild.
